Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed genitive noun phrase plus infinitive verb; ritual-cultural nominal head with listener-oriented verb complement.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To pause and consider ancestral wisdom, elders’ counsel, or inherited moral knowledge before acting.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: ᤀᤠᤍᤠᤱᤒᤠᤜᤠ᤹ᤖᤧ ᤐᤠ᤺ᤴ ᤂᤧᤵᤛᤢᤔᤠ ᤏᤢ ᤔᤡᤔ᤻ ᤆᤥ᤺ᤰᤔᤠ
Usage Contexts: family counsel; ritual speech; community meetings; ethical reflection; education
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Evokes Mundhum as ancestral oral knowledge and moral memory in Yakthung/Limbu cultural life.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Locative body-part phrase plus existential predicate; compact metaphorical clause.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To understand what is unsaid; to read emotion, intention, or hidden meaning from expression and context.
Usage Contexts: family; negotiation; friendship; literature; quiet disagreement
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Connected to indirect, respectful communication where elders or peers may imply meaning rather than state it bluntly.
Third Example Sentence: ᤆᤢᤶᤗᤢᤱᤀᤥ ᤔᤡᤰᤁᤡᤛᤥ ᤔᤡᤔ᤻ ᤘᤠ᤹ᤒᤠ ᤕᤠᤵᤔᤡ ᤁᤧᤕᤢᤱᤒᤠ॥

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Balanced parallel nominal-adjectival phrase; antithetical evaluative proverb.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Material success matters less than good judgment, generosity, and moral character.
Usage Contexts: business; family advice; marriage talk; ethics; youth guidance
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects agrarian and community-centered values where prestige is judged by conduct, not only wealth.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Reduplicated adverbial expression plus infinitive verb; iterative aspectual meaning.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To learn, understand, or resolve a matter patiently through repeated listening.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: ᤕᤠᤗᤡᤰ ᤂᤧᤵᤛᤢᤔᤠ ᤏᤢ ᤏᤢᤖᤡᤰ ᤔᤡᤔ᤻ ᤆᤥ᤺ᤰᤔᤠ
Usage Contexts: classroom; conflict mediation; child guidance; listening practice
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Echoes oral-learning habits where songs, ritual knowledge, and community histories are absorbed through repetition.
First Example Sentence: ᤆᤢᤒᤣᤕ ᤆᤢᤒᤣᤕ ᤂᤧᤵᤛᤢᤔᤠ, ᤕᤠᤰᤌᤢᤱ ᤐᤠ᤺ᤴ ᤏᤢᤖᤡᤰ ᤐᤥ᤺ᤱᤗᤥ॥
Second Example Sentence: ᤀᤠᤔᤧᤴ ᤆᤢᤒᤣᤕ ᤆᤢᤒᤣᤕ ᤂᤧᤵᤛᤢᤔᤠ ᤔᤧᤋᤋᤢ᤹, ᤂᤧᤏᤧ᤹ ᤔᤧᤜᤡᤶᤏᤧᤴ॥
Third Example Sentence: ᤛᤠᤶᤗᤥ ᤆᤢᤒᤣᤕ ᤆᤢᤒᤣᤕ ᤂᤧᤵᤛᤢᤔᤠᤀᤥ ᤏᤡᤱᤘᤠ ᤔᤧᤘᤠ᤹॥

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Coordinated natural-element noun phrase plus inchoative verb; metaphor of rooted becoming.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To become deeply rooted in a place, family, or community through shared life and responsibility.
Usage Contexts: migration; land; belonging; family settlement; rural identity
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Draws on hill agrarian imagery of soil and water as the basis of household, harvest, and belonging.
First Example Sentence: ᤀᤠᤏᤡ ᤁᤴ ᤗᤠᤈᤣ᤹ᤀᤥ ᤂᤠᤶ ᤏᤢ ᤆᤢᤘᤠ ᤐᤥ᤺ᤱᤔᤠ ᤆᤥ᤺ᤰᤗᤥ॥

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Possessive-like noun phrase plus existential predicate; personification of a community.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A community has collective memory, reputation, and moral judgment; public conduct is never fully private.
Usage Contexts: politics; village meetings; social accountability; public disputes; satire
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in village assembly culture and collective reputation in Limbuwan-area social life.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Body-part noun plus benefactive/transitive verb; conventionalized action metaphor.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To help someone practically, especially by sharing work, time, or social support.
Usage Contexts: daily speech; work; family; farming; volunteering
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects reciprocal labor traditions, household cooperation, and communal support during planting, harvest, or ceremonies.
Second Example Sentence: ᤂᤧᤏᤧ᤹ ᤁᤢᤔᤢᤰ ᤐᤡᤖᤢᤔᤠᤖᤧ ᤆᤢᤶᤒᤣᤕᤜᤠ᤹ ᤏᤥᤃᤧᤴ ᤆᤥ᤺ᤰᤋᤢ᤹॥
Third Example Sentence: ᤆᤠᤛᤥᤰᤀᤥ ᤁᤧᤖᤧᤰᤁᤧ ᤁᤢᤔᤢᤰ ᤐᤡᤖᤢᤔᤠ ᤏᤢᤖᤡᤰ ᤐᤥ᤺ᤱ॥

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Object noun plus comitative/instrumental modifier and light verb; politeness-sensitive verbal idiom.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To speak thoughtfully, respectfully, and with social care rather than carelessly or harshly.
Usage Contexts: workplace; dispute resolution; teaching; public speaking; elder-child speech
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Tied to respect for elders and the value of measured speech in household and community settings.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Numeral-derived locative phrase plus inchoative verb; collective-state metaphor.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To unite, reconcile, or act with one shared purpose despite different views.
Usage Contexts: teamwork; family reconciliation; school; community festivals; political appeals
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects collective gathering and cooperative action in household, village, and cultural events.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Cultural-locative phrase plus abstract subject and inchoative predicate; extended ceremonial metaphor.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To achieve harmony through shared rhythm, public participation, and mutual respect.
Usage Contexts: literature; cultural commentary; festivals; peace-building; speeches
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Invokes Dhan Nach/harvest-dance imagery as a symbol of synchronized communal life and cultural continuity.
Second Example Sentence: ᤛᤠᤶᤗᤥ ᤏᤢ ᤀᤡᤱᤓᤥᤖᤧ ᤎᤠᤏ ᤏᤠᤆᤀᤥ ᤏᤡᤱᤘᤠ ᤌᤡᤰᤘᤣ᤹ᤀᤥ ᤐᤥ᤺ᤱᤔᤠ ᤐᤡᤖᤢ᤹॥
Third Example Sentence: ᤆᤠᤛᤥᤰ ᤋᤠᤱᤏᤠᤶᤀᤥ ᤎᤠᤏ ᤏᤠᤆᤀᤥ ᤏᤡᤱᤘᤠ ᤌᤡᤰᤘᤣ᤹ᤀᤥ ᤐᤥ᤺ᤱᤔᤠ ᤂᤥᤛᤢᤶᤗᤥ॥
